commands.review_summary:
The PR narrows security-audit multi-user warning heuristics, downgrades constrained hook session-key override warnings to info, and updates related security-audit tests and CLI docs.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +161, Docs 0. Total +204 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path for the current false positives: current main warns whenever hook session-key override is enabled and fires the multi-user heuristic on allowlist-with-targets. I did not execute tests because this was a read-only review, but the PR body also supplies after-fix collector terminal output for the changed paths.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blockers are narrow docs/test repairs that automation can attempt before maintainer security review.

target.title: fix(security/audit): stop firing two false-positive WARNs on single-operator setups (#26859)
target.body:
## Summary

The security audit fired two false-positive `WARN`s on legitimate single-operator multi-agent setups:

1. The multi-user heuristic (`security.trust_model.multi_user_heuristic`) warned whenever a scoped allowlist with configured group targets was present — even with no broadening signal. Running several agents under `channels.<channel>.accounts.<id>` with allowlisted group targets is a normal single-operator pattern and should not trip the heuristic on its own.
2. The hook request-session-key override warned at full severity even when `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes` already constrained which session keys an external caller can choose.

This PR refactors `listPotentialMultiUserSignals` to distinguish **broadening** signals (open `dmPolicy`/`groupPolicy`, wildcard `allowFrom`/`groupAllowFrom`) from **scoped** ones (allowlist-with-targets). The heuristic now fires only when at least one broadening signal is present; the scoped signal is still surfaced in the finding detail. The constrained request-session-key case is downgraded to `info` while the unconstrained case still warns (or is critical when the gateway is remotely exposed).

Docs updated to match the new severity semantics (`docs/cli/security.md`) and the audit-check catalog (`docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md`, `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` now `info/warn/critical`). Addressed the review P3 by having each table-driven test case assert against its own `cfg` rather than a hardcoded `cases[N]` index.

Rebased onto current `main` (was ~12k commits behind); resolved the trust-model test and docs conflicts and dropped the contributor `CHANGELOG.md` entry per repo policy.

Fixes #26859.

## Real behavior proof

Workspace deps are installed, so I ran the **real** audit collector functions (`collectLikelyMultiUserSetupFindings`, `collectHooksHardeningFindings`) against real configs covering both the newly-suppressed and still-warning cases.

Behavior addressed: the audit fired two false-positive WARNs on single-operator multi-agent setups (scoped allowlist; prefix-constrained session-key override).
Real environment tested: macOS, node 22.22.2, openclaw source at the rebased-onto-main HEAD. Ran the real audit collectors via tsx against four configs.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `node --import tsx` over a harness that calls the real `collectLikelyMultiUserSetupFindings` and `collectHooksHardeningFindings` collectors against four representative configs.
Evidence after fix: live collector output below.

```
$ node --import tsx audit-live-proof.mts
A single-operator allowlist-with-targets  -> multi_user_heuristic findings: 0
B allowlist + open dmPolicy (broadening)   -> multi_user_heuristic findings: 1   [warn] security.trust_model.multi_user_heuristic
C request-session-key WITH prefixes        ->   [info] hooks.request_session_key_enabled
D request-session-key WITHOUT prefixes     ->   [warn] hooks.request_session_key_enabled   [warn] hooks.request_session_key_prefixes_missing
```

Observed result after fix: the previously false-positive single-operator allowlist-with-targets case (A) now produces zero heuristic findings, while a real broadening signal (B, open `dmPolicy`) still warns. The prefix-constrained session-key override (C) is downgraded to `info`, while the unconstrained case (D) still warns and additionally flags the missing prefixes. This matches both the "newly suppressed" and "still warning" cases requested in review.
What was not tested: a full `openclaw security audit` CLI bootstrap end-to-end was not transpiled live in this checkout; the focused run exercises the exact collector functions the CLI invokes, and the docs/catalog changes are descriptive of that behavior.

## Automated tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/security/audit-trust-model.test.ts src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts src/security/dangerous-config-flags.test.ts
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  15 passed (15)
```

## Risks

Medium (security-audit semantics): this intentionally narrows one heuristic and downgrades one finding from `warn` to `info`. Both changes are bounded — the heuristic still fires on any genuine broadening signal, and the session-key downgrade only applies when `allowedSessionKeyPrefixes` constrains the override. Maintainer review of the reduced warning surface is appropriate.

No `CHANGELOG.md` entry, per repo policy (release-owned).
target.author: koshaji
